turns.text: editmore_vertUserYou are analyzing a YouTube video transcript. Extract and structure the following information as valid JSON only (no markdown, no extra text).Return ONLY this JSON structure:{"summary": "2-3 sentence summary of the video content","keyPoints": ["point 1", "point 2", ...],"aiConcepts": ["AI concept 1", "AI concept 2", ...],"technicalDetails": ["tool/framework 1", "implementation detail", ...],"visualContextFlags": [{"timestamp": 120, "reason": "Code demo", "context": "Shows Python implementation"}]}If the video is not about AI/tech, set aiConcepts and technicalDetails to empty arrays but still extract keyPoints.TRANSCRIPT:Mixel the mixture of experts model from M AI has gone mainstream see I a big fan of AI going mainstream it shouldn't be something that a few enthusiasts are discussing it should be something that everybody can use it and one of the ways that everybody can use AI is when AI gets integrated within everyday product but if you see most of the products that in integrate AI currently at least are all open AI apis you see any Microsoft product that is all open AI stuff or most of the product Builders also use open aai so I'm always looking for things where it is not open AI or some open source model that people have integrated within their product and that is where I came across this thing Brave Leo the AI browser assistant Now features mixl for improvement performance for those who do not know I've been using Brave browser for a very very long time and also as a disclaimer definitely this is not a sponsored video no product wants to give me money so don't think that this is a sponsored video so now Brave has a product called Brave Leo and that is like the eii assistant previously they were using Lama 270 billion parameter model and now they have decided to use mixl 8x7 billion parameter model if you're completely new for mixl mixl is a mixture of experts model it's a sparse mixture of experts model where you replace one particular component from the Transformers architecture with a bunch of exports together where for every token two of the eight experts are selected to process the token and then you get the result finally and this is one of the best open source models out there today and in fact it is better than 70 billion parameter model and it is almost the chat GPT 3.5 turbo equivalent model not necessarily better than that but Char GPT 3.5 turbo equivalent model mixl has been in the news since the day it has been released since the day mistal or they release the torrent but nevertheless it is one of the best models that we have got out there so what Brave the company whose primary poster is that they are a privacy preserving browser has decided to do is they decided to use mixl so brave is integrated with Mixel or Mixel is integrated with brave in two different forms one is the brave Leo which I'll show you quickly the second one is something that they call code llm so when you go to Brave and then ask this is Brave search they also have got a search engine when you go to search. brave.com and ask a question there is a small uh toggle there at the top that would ask you if you want to generate the answer from the llm like Ai and you can generate it and then it would directly give you the answer so the reason why this is all exciting is one this is all hosted by Brave and then run it you don't need GPU the free version I think still has some rate limits that I said I don't know what kind of rate limits after how many prompts you are going to get rate limit but you can just go ahead and use it here if you have got a programming question let me first quickly show you the code llm demo then we'll jump into the Leo demo and then we'll see how it goes so the first one is you can go here to search. brave.com and then ask any question that you want so I'm going to go to search. brave.com and I'm going to say uh how to install Docker on windows so this is a question that I've asked this doesn't seem like a programming question so it doesn't give me any option here to uh let's say ask ask an llm but instead of this I'm going to say how to make a bar chart in matte plot lip this is definitely a programming related question and hence you have powered by Brave AI which is actually mixl 8x7 billion so you can click generate answer here and that would ultimately generate the answer which mixl would generate if you have self-hosted mixl so you can see it has given you the prompt it has given you the code and Sample data and and everything else that you want to do to build a bar chart yourself and it also gives you a confidence in the answer which is quite good for you to build that trust with the model I think the confidence comes from mostly like the top K value or the top P value I'm not very sure how they build the confidence but you can see it is quite useful if you have got an uh if you have got a question that is exactly a programming related question and you can ask your search engine in itself that's the whole point they've integrated now coming to Brave Leo Brave Leo can do much more than what the search engine code llm does so brave Leo is integrated within the sidebar of your browser which means you can ask a bunch of questions to give you a quick sample there is a video this is uh this is a video about a US comedy show and this video does not have any transcription mentioned here so you can go here and then see the transcript definitely if you want but if you want to get The quick summary of the video you can go click Leo and then say uh give me a summary and then it will actually give you the summary summarize this page it gives you 1 2 3 4 five 6 points in fact uh to highlight one of the things that is present in this which is uh which is NSFW uh so if you see the speaker admires the person they're talking to for not minding being Dash to people for truly not giving a reaction to potential backlash so this is something that wouldn't ever have come out of open AI APA because we all know open a AP is sensored I'm not talking about whether sensoring is good or not I'm just saying that the fact that they are using Mixel kind of model it shows that this is coming out as a um text out of this API U mixt API in this case I guess mostly they are using the transcript here in this video to do this thing but anyways despite all these things it is quite helpful you can ask question it is not just this you can go to any page for example you can go to this page and then you can just say summarize and for the first time you have to say accept and begin and then it is going to give you the summary if you have got any follow-up questions you can ask the question so for example I can go here and ask who created Cuda and this is a question I've asked so I hope it would answer like Nvidia created Cuda so yeah Cuda was created by Nvidia so you can basically chat with the page it is almost the same like all the chat with PDF stuff but you can chat with web page you can kind of chat with YouTube videos given that the transcript exist and other than that you can also go go ahead and then ask any question for example you can go to one of your search bars like you can go to the search bar here and then for example I can go ahead and then ask this question for example I can say how to build uh an Android app let's say just simply here you would see ask Leo and I can click ask Leo here and then the question is not directed to the search engine the question is now directed to the browser llm in this case mix and it is going to give you the answer so maybe this is not a really good question to ask an llm and then get an answer but you get the idea so so far what we have seen is we have seen three different ways mixl is integrated within Brave one is the code llm which is in the brave search engine the second one is within the Leo where you can like go ahead and then ask the go to the sidebar and ask any question and the third one is you can go here and uh search within the search engine and then it would give you the answer but the reason I'm excited about is not all these things primarily because Mixel is really good model and the fact that there is another very popular big company deciding to use mixol API also to I know bra is doing it because they want to enhance their stand as a privacy preserving browser but also the fact that to see mixl just being a hobbyist product into a mainstream fully productionize solution in the popular browser I don't know how many of you use brave but I consider it to be a popular browser it is really very heartening to see that Ai and mistl is going mainstream so I just felt really happy and I wanted to share this with all of you please let me know if you have any comments or uh any feedback and if you have seen these kind of eii solution in everyday product not a paid one free ones please let me know in the comment section I would definitely love to check it out see you in another video Happy prompting Mixel the mixture of experts model from M AI has gone mainstream see I a big fan of AI going mainstream it shouldn't be something that a few enthusiasts more_vertModel Thoughts Defining Data Extraction ScopeI'm currently focused on extracting and structuring specific information from a YouTube video transcript. My goal is to extract key elements like summaries, key points, relevant AI concepts, technical details, and any visual context flags. All of this has to be formatted into valid JSON, with no markdown or extraneous text. I'm working with a transcript that discusses...Analyzing Brave's Mixtral IntegrationI've zeroed in on the specifics of Brave's Mixtral integration. The main points I am focusing on are the shift from Llama 2 to the 8x7B Mixtral model, and how it is a Mixture of Experts (MoE) model. I've also noted features like web page/video summarization, and programming answers with confidence scores. The core integration points are Brave Leo and Brave Search Code LLM, which are also worth my time to focus on.Identifying Visual ContextI'm now focusing on the visual cues in the video. I've pinpointed specific timestamps highlighting Code LLM in action on search.brave.com, Matplotlib code generation, the Leo sidebar with a video summary, and a demonstration of chatting with a webpage about CUDA/NVIDIA, which further clarifies and validates my understanding of the features and capabilities of the integration.Detailing Brave Leo CapabilitiesI'm now zeroing in on the functionality of Brave Leo, specifically its ability to summarize both YouTube videos and web pages directly within the browser. I've noted the free-to-use nature of the service, although rate limits are in place. The core AI concepts like Mixture of Experts and the Mixtral 8x7B architecture are clearly relevant. I'm also considering the implications of the underlying technology like Sparse MoE, Transformers, Llama 2, and token processing. I'm taking notes on how it leverages confidence scores, and any technical demonstrations that I've found. I am very aware of the need to exclude markdown code blocks in my JSON output and that I should ensure all the JSON is valid. Expand to view model thoughts  chevron_right

editmore_vert{"summary": "This video explores the integration of the Mixtral 8x7B Mixture of Experts model into the Brave browser's AI assistant, Leo, and its search engine. It highlights how this shift from Llama 2 provides users with high-performance, privacy-focused AI capabilities for summarization and coding assistance directly within the browser.","keyPoints": ["Brave has updated its AI assistant, Leo, to use the Mixtral 8x7B model, replacing Llama 2 70B.","The integration is available in two forms: Brave Leo (sidebar assistant) and Code LLM (integrated into Brave Search).","Mixtral 8x7B is a Sparse Mixture of Experts model that performs at a level equivalent to GPT-3.5 Turbo.","Leo can summarize web pages and YouTube videos (via transcripts) and handle follow-up questions.","The features are hosted by Brave, meaning users do not need a local GPU to run the models.","Brave Search's Code LLM provides programming answers with confidence scores based on top K/P values."],"aiConcepts": ["Mixture of Experts (MoE)","Sparse Mixture of Experts","Transformers architecture","Large Language Models (LLM)","Token processing","Model quantization/scaling","Prompt engineering"],"technicalDetails": ["Mixtral 8x7B","Llama 2 70B","Brave Leo","Brave Search (search.brave.com)","Code LLM","Matplotlib implementation","Confidence scores (Top K/Top P)"],"visualContextFlags": [{"timestamp": 160, "reason": "Code LLM demo", "context": "Shows Brave Search generating Matplotlib code for a bar chart."},{"timestamp": 225, "reason": "Brave Leo UI", "context": "Demonstrates the sidebar assistant summarizing a YouTube video transcript."},{"timestamp": 281, "reason": "Web page chat", "context": "Shows the user asking specific technical questions about NVIDIA and CUDA based on page content."}]}thumb_upthumb_down
